key_insights.insight: Humans have at least 34 senses, not just the traditionally recognized five, which expands our potential for understanding and interacting with the world.
key_insights.quote: I actually did a literature review of several pieces of research about how many senses humans have. And we actually have 34, as we currently understand it.

key_insights.insight: The brain filters the capabilities of the mind to allow us to function on the material plane.
key_insights.quote: I believe that the brain actually filters down the capability of the mind so that we can exist on this material plane.

key_insights.insight: Grief can manifest physically and emotionally, sometimes mirroring symptoms of psychosis due to changes in neurotransmitter levels.
key_insights.quote: If you're going through grief and you don't know the things that I know, you can't articulate to yourself that grief in many ways is like psychosis.

key_insights.insight: Trauma can be stored in the body and require somatic or physical therapies to release it, because it resides outside the realm of verbal processing.
key_insights.quote: There may be residual trauma that's held in your body that you can't actually articulate and get out and solve through talking therapy.So it requires some kind of physical therapy.

key_insights.insight: There is a potential for humans to communicate with those that have passed away.
key_insights.quote: I couldn't speak about it until now, that it's possible to communicate with someone that's passed away.And I'm saying it from the point of view of being a neuroscientist and a psychiatrist.

key_insights.insight: Experiencing or even learning about near-death experiences can lead to increased compassion, gratitude, and decreased materialism.
key_insights.quote: Students from various different areas of expertise who simply learn about near-death experiences actually get some of the same benefits and that these can last for over a year later.

key_insights.insight: True connection involves a deep, meaningful, and altruistic bond with oneself, others, and something greater than oneself.
key_insights.quote: It's about connection to yourself to others and, you know, sort of the world or something greater. And, you know, it's about something that's deep and not transactional. It's about something that's meaningful. It's about something that's altruistic.

key_insights.insight: Hidden wisdom is also stored in the body, it's not just in your brain.
key_insights.quote: Hidden wisdom is also stored in the body, it's not just in your brain.

actionable_advice.advice: Engage in somatic work to release trauma held in the body.
actionable_advice.how_to: Participate in activities like massage, dance, art therapy, craniosacral therapy, or Tai Chi.

actionable_advice.advice: Cultivate your ability to notice beauty to improve your overall well-being.
actionable_advice.how_to: Actively seek out beautiful things in your environment and actively focus on them, creating a habit of heightened awareness and oxytocin release.

actionable_advice.advice: Strengthen gut health to sharpen intuition and reduce inflammation in the brain.
actionable_advice.how_to: Improve your diet, take prebiotics and probiotics, and reduce inflammation through exercise, stress management, and even meditation, art, or music therapy.

actionable_advice.advice: Be open to receiving signs from the universe and loved ones that have passed away.
actionable_advice.how_to: Actively ask for signs, look for patterns and coincidences, and interpret their meanings in a personal and meaningful way.
Spend time in complete darkness for several days to a few weeks, meditating and doing breathwork.

memorable_stories: Dr. Swart's vivid encounter with her deceased husband's apparition.
The story of the patient in ICU who had a near-death experience and accurately described the nurse's tragic death and details of her red MG.
Dr. Swart's story of walking past the hospital where her husband received treatment and receiving an elastic band in the shape of an infinity symbol as a sign.
contrarian_claims: Humans possess at least 34 senses, significantly more than the commonly accepted five.
Grief is akin to psychosis in its effects on brain chemistry and neurotransmitter levels.
The mind and body can operate independently of each other.
The ability to connect with the deceased is possible and can be enhanced through specific practices.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Neuroscience Expert Dr. Tara Swart on Evidence We Can Communicate After Death and Her Experience Speaking to the Dead!
